incidents.desc: SAP overnight batch processing stalled — 847 FI documents queued. Dialog work process WP-04 holding BKPF/BSEG table lock for 27 minutes, blocking job cascade. Finance, AR, bank statement import and procurement approval all halted.
incidents.notes: ZeroOps auto-resolved: stale session cancelled, lock released, all 4 downstream jobs restarted. 847 documents processed. Month-end close unaffected. SM04 lock age monitoring added to prevent recurrence.

intel.rootCause: SAP dialog work process WP-04 holding BKPF/BSEG table lock for 27 minutes — stale BATCH_FI session not released, blocking RFBIBL00 background job and cascading to 3 downstream jobs
intel.recommendation: Stale session cancelled autonomously. New SM04 lock age alert added at 10-minute threshold. KEDB updated KB-AG-SAP-001.

cause.primary: Stale dialog session BATCH_FI holding BKPF table lock — background job cascade blocked
cause.mech: SAP dialog work process WP-04 acquired a lock on the BKPF/BSEG tables during FI document posting. The session became unresponsive but did not release the lock. The background job RFBIBL00 (document posting) was waiting on this lock, causing a cascade: RFDABL00 (AR), RFEBBU00 (bank statements) and MM60 (procurement) all queued behind it.

chain.d: Unresponsive — holding BKPF/BSEG lock for 27 min
Stalled — waiting on table lock — 847 documents queued
All blocked — cascade downstream jobs queued
Invoice generation, bank import, PO approval all halted

signals.v: sap_batch_queue_depth 847 — threshold 500 breached
SAP work process utilisation 94% — dialog pool saturated
RFBIBL00 ACTIVE 27 min with no progress — lock wait confirmed
Pattern match: INC0001987 · 91% similarity · same WP lock pattern

similar.res: Manual SM04 session cancel by BASIS team
SAP restart required — lock not released

runbook.title: Identify stale lock via SM04
runbook.cmd: RFC call: SUSR_USER_LOGOFF · SM04 session list
runbook.note: Lock age > 10 min flagged as stale

runbook.title: Validate downstream jobs
runbook.cmd: SM37: confirm RFDABL00, RFEBBU00, MM60 ACTIVE

incidents.desc: PACS application server JVM heap exhausted — DICOM receiver unresolved heap leak. 847 image retrievals failing. Radiology workflow impacted across 6 hospital sites.
incidents.notes: Heap at 98%. OOM kills every 4-7 minutes. Rolling restart partial relief only. Code fix required in DICOM receiver thread pool.

intel.rootCause: PACS JVM heap exhausted — DICOM receiver thread pool object reference leak in DicomReceiverThread.java causing linear heap growth
intel.recommendation: Rolling restart with -Xmx12g patch applied. Code fix tracked in ServiceNow CHG-8821. 30-day monitoring window active.

cause.primary: DicomReceiverThread.java object reference leak — heap growing 0.8% per minute
cause.mech: DICOM receiver threads are not releasing dataset objects after processing. Linear heap growth of 0.8% per minute leads to OOM kills every 4-7 minutes. 6 hospital sites losing image retrieval capability.

chain.d: Object references not released in finally block
Radiologists unable to retrieve images — workflow halted

signals.v: heap_used_ratio > 0.95 on pacs-app-prod-01 — OOM kill imminent
DICOM retrieval success rate dropped to 12% — 847 failures
DicomReceiverThread FATAL — object retention leak pattern confirmed
186 Prometheus alerts correlated → 1 P1 incident raised
6 hospital site connections degraded — radiology workflow impacted

similar.res: Gateway restart and queue replay — 847 messages delivered
JVM heap -Xmx increase as interim fix

runbook.title: Rolling restart
runbook.note: Zero-downtime — one pod at a time

runbook.title: Apply heap config patch
runbook.note: Interim relief while code fix developed

incidents.desc: HL7 ADT/ORM message queue backing up — 2,847 messages unprocessed. Patient admission and order routing delayed across all connected hospital sites.
incidents.notes: Consumer thread deadlock under morning peak load. Queue growing at 340 messages/min. Patient data flow blocked.

intel.rootCause: HL7 Gateway consumer thread deadlock — DB connection pool exhaustion under morning peak load causing 2,847 message queue backup
intel.recommendation: HiTL gateway restart approved. Queue replayed. DB connection pool increase to 50 recommended to prevent recurrence.

cause.primary: HL7 consumer thread deadlock — DB connection pool exhausted at 20 connections under peak load
cause.mech: Morning peak load of 340 HL7 messages/minute exceeded the DB connection pool limit of 20 connections. Threads entered deadlock waiting for connections. Queue backed up to 2,847 messages blocking all patient ADT/ORM flows.

chain.d: 20 connections exhausted — threads waiting on lock
All hospital admission and order routing blocked

signals.v: hl7_queue_depth > 2,000 — consumer threads not draining
HL7 consumer threads BLOCKED — DB connection lock contention
Thread dump: 12/12 consumer threads in BLOCKED state
P1 raised — patient data flow blocked across all sites
Root cause: DB pool exhaustion — matches INC0002065 pattern (88% sim)

similar.res: Gateway restart and queue replay — same deadlock pattern

runbook.title: Confirm thread deadlock
runbook.cmd: kubectl exec hl7-gateway -- jstack | grep BLOCKED

runbook.title: HiTL approval for restart
runbook.cmd: ServiceNow HiTL — patient data flow impact

runbook.title: Restart HL7 Gateway
runbook.note: Clears thread deadlock and connection pool

runbook.title: Replay message queue
runbook.note: Deliver all queued messages in order

incidents.desc: DICOM archive partition /vna at 91% capacity. Study ingest will fail at 95% — estimated 4 hours at current ingestion rate.
incidents.notes: Auto-cleanup scheduled but insufficient. Emergency expansion or archive tier migration required.

intel.rootCause: DICOM archive partition /vna at 91% capacity — ingest failure imminent at 95% within 4 hours at current 2.4 TB/day rate
intel.recommendation: Emergency Azure Blob tier migration for studies > 2 years. NFS expansion via Azure NetApp Files as secondary option.

cause.primary: VNA storage partition /vna at 91% — breach at 95% in approximately 4 hours
cause.mech: DICOM study ingest at 2.4 TB/day has exhausted scheduled cleanup cycles. Partition will reach 95% threshold — at which point all new study ingest will fail — within 4 hours at current rate.

chain.d: 91% full — 4h to breach at current rate
Will fail at 95% — radiology workflow blocked
Old studies not migrated — no headroom created

signals.v: vna_partition_used_ratio 0.91 — above 0.90 critical threshold
Growth trajectory: breach at 95% in 3.8 hours at 2.4TB/day
Azure Blob tier eligible studies: 47TB — immediate migration available

runbook.title: Monitor partition headroom
runbook.note: Alert if crosses 93% before migration completes

runbook.title: Update capacity thresholds
runbook.note: Earlier warning at 80% gives 8-day lead time

incidents.desc: RUBEE Orchestrator thread pool 85% saturated at morning peak. Worklist distribution to radiologists delayed by 8-12 minutes.
incidents.notes: Thread pool ceiling 200 workers. Peak load hitting 170+. Auto-scale config not applied after last maintenance window.

intel.rootCause: RUBEE Orchestrator thread pool 85% saturated — auto-scale config not applied after last maintenance window
intel.recommendation: Apply Kubernetes HPA for RUBEE deployment. Immediate thread pool increase to 400 pending HiTL approval.

cause.primary: RUBEE thread pool ceiling 200 workers — auto-scale config removed during maintenance
cause.mech: During last maintenance window the Kubernetes HPA config was not reapplied. Thread pool ceiling of 200 workers is insufficient for morning peak of 170+ concurrent worklist requests. Radiologist worklist distribution delayed 8-12 minutes.

chain.d: HPA config not reapplied — auto-scale disabled

signals.v: rubee_thread_pool_utilisation 85% — above 80% alert threshold
Worklist distribution latency 8.4 min — SLA is 2 min
RUBEE worker thread queue depth growing — HPA not active

similar.res: Manual thread pool increase to 300 — resolved within 1h

incidents.desc: VNA archive replication lag 14 seconds — threshold is 5 seconds. Study retrieval from long-term archive intermittently failing for studies older than 2 years.
incidents.notes: Replication slot accumulating WAL. Similar to INC0002065 pattern — inactive slot not cleaned up after maintenance.

intel.rootCause: PostgreSQL replication slot accumulating WAL — inactive slot left after maintenance causing 14-second replication lag
intel.recommendation: Drop inactive replication slot, trigger CHECKPOINT, monitor lag return to < 5 seconds.

cause.primary: Inactive PostgreSQL replication slot accumulating WAL — lag 14s vs 5s SLA
cause.mech: An inactive replication slot was left after the last maintenance window. PostgreSQL retains WAL segments for all active slots — including inactive ones. WAL accumulation is causing replication lag of 14 seconds, intermittently blocking retrieval of studies older than 2 years.

chain.d: Replication slot not cleaned up — left inactive
PostgreSQL retaining WAL for inactive slot

signals.v: pg_replication_lag_seconds 14.2 — above 5s SLA threshold
pg_replication_slots: slot_name=lta_replica active=false — WAL accumulating
LTA study retrieval: 34% failure rate on studies older than 2 years

similar.res: Dropped inactive slot, CHECKPOINT — lag resolved in 8 min

runbook.title: Identify inactive slot
runbook.cmd: psql: SELECT slot_name, active FROM pg_replication_slots WHERE active=false

runbook.title: Trigger CHECKPOINT
runbook.note: Flush WAL backlog — lag should resolve within 10 min

runbook.title: Monitor replication lag
runbook.note: Confirm return to SLA within 15 min

incidents.desc: Morning worklist queue depth reached 1,204 studies — above 1,000 alert threshold. Auto-rebalanced across 3 reading stations.
incidents.notes: ZeroOps auto-resolved: rebalanced queue in 4 min 22 sec. No radiologist impact.

intel.rootCause: Worklist queue depth reached 1,204 studies — morning peak exceeded single reading station capacity. Auto-rebalanced by ZeroOps.
intel.recommendation: Resolved autonomously. Consider pre-emptive rebalancing trigger at 800 studies to provide earlier relief.

cause.primary: Worklist queue depth 1,204 — morning peak overwhelmed single station assignment
cause.mech: Morning radiology peak caused single reading station to accumulate 1,204 studies in queue. ZeroOps detected the depth breach and auto-rebalanced across 3 reading stations in 4 minutes 22 seconds. No radiologist impact.

chain.d: Study volume 3x normal — single station assignment
Queue depth normalised — no radiologist impact

signals.v: worklist_queue_depth 1,204 — above 1,000 alert threshold
Auto-rebalance triggered — distributing across 3 reading stations
Queue depth 412 — within normal range. Auto-resolved.

incidents.desc: FHIR R4 API returning 503 errors on /ImagingStudy endpoint. 47 API consumers affected for 6 minutes.
incidents.notes: ZeroOps auto-resolved: pod restart triggered in 3 min 18 sec. Root cause: memory pressure from large DICOM metadata payload.

intel.rootCause: FHIR R4 API pod OOMKill — memory pressure from large DICOM metadata payload causing 503 errors on /ImagingStudy endpoint
intel.recommendation: Pod restarted — resolved. Memory limit increase from 512Mi to 1Gi recommended to prevent recurrence.

cause.primary: FHIR API pod OOMKilled — large DICOM metadata payload exceeded 512Mi memory limit
cause.mech: A large DICOM metadata payload on the /ImagingStudy endpoint caused the FHIR API pod to exceed its 512Mi memory limit. OOMKill triggered. ZeroOps detected the pod failure and executed an automatic restart in 3 minutes 18 seconds.

chain.d: ImagingStudy query returning metadata for 2,400+ studies

signals.v: fhir_api_error_rate 100% — pod OOMKilled
FHIR API 503 errors — pod restart triggered automatically
FHIR API availability 99.97% restored — 47 consumers unblocked

similar.res: Pod restart — same OOMKill pattern on large payload

incidents.desc: Radiology AI inference latency increased from 140ms to 890ms. GPU memory fragmentation after 72h continuous operation. Auto-triage accuracy dropping.
incidents.notes: Scheduled GPU restart needed. Latency SLA is 200ms. Currently at 890ms — impacting radiologist workflow.

intel.rootCause: Radiology AI GPU memory fragmentation after 72 hours continuous operation — inference latency increased from 140ms to 890ms
intel.recommendation: Scheduled GPU restart during low-activity window (02:00-04:00). Implement 72h auto-restart schedule to prevent recurrence.

cause.primary: GPU memory fragmentation after 72h continuous operation — latency 890ms vs 200ms SLA
cause.mech: The AI Inference Engine GPU accumulates memory fragmentation during continuous operation. After 72 hours without restart, fragmentation causes inference latency to increase from baseline 140ms to 890ms — 4.5x above the 200ms SLA. A scheduled restart during low-activity hours defragments GPU memory.

chain.d: 4.5x above 200ms SLA — radiologist AI triage impacted
Accuracy within range — latency only, not accuracy

signals.v: ai_inference_latency_p99 890ms — SLA 200ms (4.5x breach)
GPU memory fragmentation 94% — 72h uptime confirmed
Pattern: latency degrades linearly after 60h — schedule restart at 72h

similar.res: Scheduled GPU restart — latency restored to 140ms
similar.out: Temporary — no auto-restart schedule in place

similar.res: Emergency GPU restart — same fragmentation pattern

runbook.title: Schedule restart window
runbook.cmd: cron: kubectl rollout restart deployment/ai-inference at 02:00

incidents.desc: Multi-tenant API rate limiter misconfiguration causing 429 errors for 3 hospital tenant namespaces. 312 API calls rejected.
incidents.notes: ZeroOps auto-resolved: rate limit config corrected in 8 min 44 sec. Root cause: config drift after last deployment.

intel.rootCause: Multi-tenant API rate limiter misconfiguration — config drift after deployment caused 429 errors for 3 hospital tenant namespaces
intel.recommendation: Rate limit config corrected. Config drift monitoring enabled. GitOps deployment validation recommended.

cause.primary: API rate limiter config drift post-deployment — 3 tenant namespaces incorrectly throttled
cause.mech: A deployment of the API Gateway introduced a rate limiter config that incorrectly throttled 3 hospital tenant namespaces to 10 requests/minute instead of 1,000. ZeroOps detected the 429 error pattern and corrected the config automatically.

chain.d: Rate limiter config overwritten — 3 namespaces affected
312 API calls rejected — hospital integrations failing

signals.v: api_error_rate_429 > 50% for 3 tenant namespaces
Config drift detected — rate_limit changed from 1000 to 10 post-deployment
Rate limit restored — 429 errors cleared, all tenants operational

similar.res: Manual config rollback — took 1h 22m to diagnose
similar.out: Temporary — drift detection not in place

runbook.title: Identify affected namespaces
runbook.cmd: kubectl get configmap api-rate-limits -o yaml | grep rateLimit

runbook.title: Enable config drift monitoring
runbook.note: Alerts on any future config divergence from baseline

intel.rootCause: DICOM router AE title misconfiguration after network maintenance — 23 studies routed to incorrect reading station
intel.recommendation: AE title mapping corrected and validated. Post-maintenance config verification checklist recommended.

23 studies sent to incorrect reading station
AE title mismatch detected in DICOM router logs

AE title mapping diverged from CMDB baseline post-maintenance

runbook.note: Deliver all 23 studies to correct station

incidents.desc: CVE-2024-9812 identified in DICOM Toolkit dependency. Patch assessment and controlled upgrade required within 72h compliance window.
incidents.notes: Resolved: patch applied via controlled change window. Change Validator auto-approved risk score 18/100.

intel.rootCause: CVE-2024-9812 identified in DICOM Toolkit dependency — patch assessment and controlled upgrade required within 72h compliance window
intel.recommendation: Patch applied via controlled change window. Change Validator confirmed clean deployment. No further action required.

cause.primary: CVE-2024-9812 — CVSS 7.8 HIGH — DICOM Toolkit v3.4.1 arbitrary code execution vulnerability
cause.mech: Security scan identified CVE-2024-9812 in DICOM Toolkit v3.4.1 — a high-severity arbitrary code execution vulnerability. Compliance policy requires patch within 72 hours. ZeroOps prepared patch assessment and coordinated controlled upgrade via Change Validator.

ZeroOps coordinated patch via change window
DICOM Toolkit v3.4.2 deployed — vulnerability closed

Patch v3.4.2 available — risk score 18 — controlled upgrade recommended
Patch deployed successfully — CVE-2024-9812 closed — compliance maintained

incidents.desc: DICOM image compression throughput dropped 60% after overnight batch. Worker thread starvation under concurrent load.
incidents.notes: ZeroOps auto-resolved: thread pool rebalanced in 2 min 8 sec. No study delivery impact.

intel.rootCause: DICOM compression engine worker thread starvation — concurrent load during overnight batch caused throughput drop of 60%
intel.recommendation: Thread pool rebalanced autonomously in 2m 8s. Pre-batch thread pool pre-scaling recommended before overnight jobs.

cause.primary: Compression worker thread starvation under concurrent overnight batch load
cause.mech: Overnight batch processing and concurrent daytime compression requests competed for the same 48-worker thread pool. Batch jobs held 41 workers leaving only 7 for interactive compression — causing 60% throughput drop. ZeroOps rebalanced the thread pool in 2m 8s.

signals.v: compression_throughput_ratio 0.40 — 60% below baseline
compression_worker_available 7 — batch jobs holding 41/48 threads
Thread pool rebalanced — compression throughput restored to 100%

incidents.notes: ZeroOps auto-resolved: gateway restart and queue replay in 18 min 22 sec. Root cause: same thread pool pattern as INC0002098.

intel.rootCause: HL7 Gateway consumer thread deadlock during morning peak — same DB connection pool exhaustion pattern as INC0002098. Auto-resolved by ZeroOps.
intel.recommendation: Resolved autonomously in 18m 22s. DB connection pool increase to 50 urgently needed — same pattern recurred as INC0002098 today.

cause.primary: HL7 consumer thread deadlock — DB pool exhausted at morning peak (same as INC0002098)
cause.mech: Morning peak ADT message volume exceeded DB connection pool capacity of 20 connections. Consumer thread deadlock caused 847 messages to back up over 12 minutes. ZeroOps autonomously restarted the gateway and replayed all messages.
